title: I Stopped Storing '.m2/settings.xml' in Github Repos — Everything Got Better
description: How generating settings.xml on every CI run (instead of storing it) eliminated drift across 80+ Maven repos. A GitHub Actions composite action walkthrough.

title: Enhancing Observability in MuleSoft with Grafana
description: Set up real-time dashboards for your Mule 4 APIs using Grafana. Covers log shipping, metrics collection, and alerting for production-grade observability.

title: Automating Mule Migration from 4.4.0 to 4.6 with JDK17: Guide for Saving Time
description: A batch script that automated Mule 4.x + Java 17 migration across dozens of projects, cutting manual effort by 50%. Includes step-by-step guide.

title: Writing RAML Datatype like a PRO
description: A complete guide to writing clean, reusable RAML 1.0 DataTypes for MuleSoft APIs. Covers types, inheritance, optional fields, and common patterns.

title: How to Create a DW Script Using DW Script
description: Meta-programming in DataWeave — using DW transformations to generate other DW scripts. A creative deep-dive into DataWeave's functional capabilities.

title: Secret Agent Script: Encrypt & Decrypt Like a PRO
description: Built a local utility to encrypt and decrypt secrets without re-entering the key every time. Handy for MuleSoft secure property placeholders.

title: DataWeave Magic: Identifying Special Weekdays
description: A handy DataWeave snippet to detect business days, weekends, and holidays within date ranges. Great for scheduling flows and conditional logic.

title: Unveiling the Secrets of CloudHub's Schedulers in MuleSoft
description: Deep dive into how CloudHub handles scheduler behaviour across workers — including the quirks most developers hit in production.

title: How to Disable Schedulers in Anypoint Studio: A Developer's Guide
description: Practical tips for disabling schedulers during local development in Anypoint Studio — no more accidental DB writes while debugging.

title: Cloning an Azure Repo into GitHub Desktop with Single Click
description: A bookmarklet trick that adds a one-click 'Open in GitHub Desktop' button to Azure DevOps repo pages. Zero install, huge time saver.
